perturbation.original_text: CONSULTANT further agrees that it will not assign the responsibilities set forth in the attached Statement of Work to any other CONSULTANT, subcontactor or other individual without the express written agreement of the Company's Chief Executive Officer.
perturbation.changed_text: CONSULTANT further agrees that it may assign the responsibilities set forth in the attached Statement of Work to any other CONSULTANT, subcontactor or other individual, even without the express written agreement of the Company's Chief Executive Officer.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction because it directly conflicts with the original text. Initially, the contract strictly prohibits the CONSULTANT from assigning responsibilities without the Company's explicit written consent. This change states that the CONSULTANT may assign those responsibilities, which could potentially undermine the control and oversight the Company expects to have, contradicting Section 1, where CONSULTANT is expected to perform professionally.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Consulting Services. REIT hereby retains CONSULTANT and CONSULTANT hereby accepts appointment to serve as an independent contractor to the Company. During the term of this Agreement, CONSULTANT shall consult with REIT and provide the services outlined in the attached Statement of Work in a timely and professional manner in accordance with industry standards. CONSULTANT further agrees that it will not assign the responsibilities set forth in the attached Statement of Work to any other CONSULTANT, subcontactor or other individual without the express written agreement of the Company's Chief Executive Officer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original and contradicted text. The original text states that the CONSULTANT may NOT assign responsibilities without the express written agreement, whereas the changed text says the CONSULTANT MAY assign responsibilities even without the written agreement. This is a direct conflict and creates a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Completeness of work product shall be determined by REIT in its sole discretion, and CONSULTANT agrees to make all revisions, additions, deletions or alterations as requested by the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Completeness of work product shall be determined by CONSULTANT, in its sole discretion, and CONSULTANT has no obligation to make any revisions, additions, deletions or alterations as requested by the Company.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction in Section 4. The original clause gives REIT the sole discretion to determine the completeness of the work product and requires the CONSULTANT to make changes as requested. The modified text transfers this discretion to the CONSULTANT and removes the obligation to comply with REIT's requests. This directly undermines REIT's control over the deliverables and contradicts the expectations outlined in the original agreement. Furthermore, the change contradicts the sentence after that stating 'No other fees and/or expenses will be paid to CONSULTANT, unless such fees and/or expenses have been approved in advance by the appropriate Company executive.', since the 'work product' might now be deemed to be incomplete, as its completeness can't be decided by REIT.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation and Reimbursement. CONSULTANT shall be compensated and reimbursed for the services as set forth in the attached Statement of Work. Completeness of work product shall be determined by REIT in its sole discretion, and CONSULTANT agrees to make all revisions, additions, deletions or alterations as requested by the Company. No other fees and/or expenses will be paid to CONSULTANT, unless such fees and/or expenses have been approved in advance by the appropriate Company executive. CONSULTANT shall be solely responsible for any and all taxes, Social Security contributions or payments, disability insurance, unemployment taxes, and other payroll-type taxes applicable to such compensation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement by transferring the power to determine work product completeness from REIT to the CONSULTANT. It also removes the CONSULTANT's obligation to make revisions requested by REIT. This directly conflicts with the original text which states that 'Completeness of work product shall be determined by REIT in its sole discretion, and CONSULTANT agrees to make all revisions, additions, deletions or alterations as requested by the Company.' This alteration creates a clear and irreconcilable contradiction, as the same sentence now gives conflicting instructions.

perturbation.original_text: CONSULTANT agrees that during its consultancy for REIT and for a period of twelve (12) months immediately following the termination of its consultancy with the Company for any reason, whether with or without cause, it will not: (a) solicit, entice or induce any Customer for the purpose of providing, or provide, products or services that are competitive with the products or services provided by the Company, or (b) solicit, entice, or induce any Customer to terminate or reduce its business with (or refrain from increasing its business with) the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: CONSULTANT agrees that it is entitled to solicit, entice or induce any Customer for the purpose of providing, or provide, products or services that are competitive with the products or services provided by the Company, and is also entitled to solicit, entice, or induce any Customer to terminate or reduce its business with (or refrain from increasing its business with) the Company.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts the non-solicitation clause in Section 7.2. The original text explicitly prohibits the CONSULTANT from soliciting customers for competitive purposes or inducing them to reduce business with the Company. The modified text states the opposite, asserting that the CONSULTANT *is* entitled to engage in these activities. This undermines the Company's protection against the CONSULTANT leveraging their knowledge and relationships gained during the consultancy to harm the Company's business. This also means CONSULTANT may now be in violation of the Non-Disparagement clause, since doing all of the above, harms 'the business reputation of the Company'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disparagement. CONSULTANT acknowledges that any disparaging comments by him against the Company are likely to substantially depreciate the business reputation of the Company. CONSULTANT agrees to act in good faith so as to not harm the business reputation of the Company in any way. CONSULTANT further agrees that it will not directly or indirectly defame, disparage, or publicly criticize the services, business, integrity, veracity or reputation of the Company or its owners, officers, directors, or employees in any forum or through any medium of communication. Nothing in this Agreement will preclude CONSULTANT from supplying truthful information to any governmental authority or in response to any lawful subpoena or other legal process.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the CONSULTANT *is* entitled to solicit customers and reduce their business with the company, which directly contradicts the 'Non-Disparagement' clause. The Non-Disparagement clause states 'CONSULTANT agrees to act in good faith so as to not harm the business reputation of the Company in any way'. Soliciting customers away from the Company and inducing them to reduce business with the company would constitute harm to the business reputation, which the consultant is now permitted to do according to the changed text, creating a direct contradiction.
